title: <div>My back is sweating from peddling across the pavement of these monolithic c
content: My back is sweating from peddling across the pavement of these monolithic city as I sit in the ferry being ferried across the bay. For hours I peddled, swaying between the lines of lawful and unlawful driving, swerving between automobiles, and ambulances, and great big hatch back trucks -- where if at any moment I grew invisible in the side mirrors of the million drivers I'd be squashed, split into, molded into one with my bicycle. I'd be just another momentary cause of the further clogging of traffic in the ancient city of Manhattan, ancient yet not much more than 200 years old. Collected in its architecture and those who inhabit the architecture are thousands of years of specimen and progress collected from the collective humanity spread throughout the entirety of world.
